perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Opening Deadline. You are responsible for purchasing, leasing, or subleasing a suitable site for the Franchised Business. You agree to establish the Franchised Business and have it open and in operation within six (6) months after the Effective Date of this Agreement. Time is of the essence.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Opening Deadline. You are responsible for purchasing, leasing, or subleasing a suitable site for the Franchised Business. You agree to establish the Franchised Business and have it open and in operation within twelve (12) months after the Effective Date of this Agreement. Time is of the essence.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction, because the opening deadline for when the franchise should be open and in operation has been changed from six to twelve months after the effective date. This could create a contradiction when determining if one of the party is in default of the agreement, or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17.2.1 If you do not obtain an Approved Location for the Franchised Business within the time limits specified under the Site Selection Addendum, or if you do not construct and open the Franchised Business within the time limits specified in Sections 5.1 and 8.2 above, and within the requirements specified in Sections 5 and 8.2 above;
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the opening deadline from six to twelve months. The contradicted text, Section 17.2.1, refers to Section 5.1 regarding the time limits for opening the Franchised Business. Since the deadline in Section 5.1 has been changed, it creates a contradiction regarding which time frame (six or twelve months) should be enforced, thus impacting the determination of default under the agreement. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1.7 With regard to insurance products offered by you, the insurance carriers will set the policy prices, and we will set the Agency Fees.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1.7 With regard to insurance products offered by you, we reserve the right to set the policy prices and we will set the Agency Fees.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. By changing which party will set the policy prices, to Goosehead Insurance, conflicts with other parts of the agreement such as the language in Section 8.12 that states *you will have the right to set the prices that you will charge to your customers*.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.12 Prices. You agree that we may set reasonable restrictions on the maximum and minimum prices you may charge for the Approved Products and Services offered and sold at the Franchised Business under this Agreement. Subject to the terms of Section 7.1.7 above, you will have the right to set the prices that you will charge to your customers; provided, however, that (subject to applicable law): (a) if we have set a maximum price for a particular item, then you may charge any price for that item up to and including the maximum price we have set; and (b) if we have set a minimum price for a particular item, then you may charge any price for that item that is equal to or above the minimum price we have set.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7.1.7, stating that 'we reserve the right to set the policy prices,' directly contradicts Section 8.12, which states 'you will have the right to set the prices that you will charge to your customers.' This creates a clear conflict regarding who has the authority to set prices, leading to potential disputes and uncertainty in the agreement.
